## Target's compacted history
The target's earlier work has been folded into the `<summary>` block(s) below — this is what the target itself sees at the head of its context, NOT verbatim transcript. Use it to understand the arc: what was decided, what constraints were set, what was already done. If the window below shows the target acting on an assumption that CONTRADICTS a summary, re-doing work a summary says was completed, or ignoring a constraint a summary records — that is context drift; flag it. Do NOT trace values inside the summaries (they're compacted, not tool-verifiable). Do NOT emit `msg_idx` pointing into this section — `msg_idx` is window-relative. If a summary's paraphrase is ambiguous on the point you're checking, `query_target_history(summary=<id>, question=…)` fetches the original behind it — use sparingly.

<summary id=7upuqh7j>
Faber2026 repo cleanup, ponytail audit review, and reproducibility-spine build (2026-07-08)

## Context & repo facts
Working repo: `/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026` (rw grant). It is a git repo (456 commits, first 2026-06-18, current branch `docs/gate-f-readability-pass`). **Critical structural fact discovered and used throughout:** `pipeline/` is NOT a directory — it is a git **submodule** pointing at `https://github.com/dsa110-FLITS.git` (`.gitmodules`: `url = https://github.com/dsa110/dsa110-FLITS.git`; the manuscript's Data Availability and `\software` both cite `https://github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS`). Any edit under `pipeline/…` is a change to that shared library, not manuscript housekeeping. Frame ID `16d37508-9382-4535-9ccc-7c7827a895f7`, project `proj_55f9c893cfe1`. Agent workspace: `/Users/jakobfaber/.claude-science/orgs/3dfe4daa-34a3-475b-a7d9-194ed338c8cc/workspaces/16d37508-9382-4535-9ccc-7c7827a895f7`.

## Phase 1 — ponytail audit review (e1–e15)
User attached `pasted-text-2026-07-08T19-29-12.txt` (version_id `0be6c6f1-141d-4808-9928-3e81ef2cbc05`, artifact 5257 bytes): a 30-item "Ponytail audit — Faber2026 (refreshed)" recommending ~-14,200 lines, -4 deps of deletions/refactors. Agent verified claims against disk rather than riffing.

Key findings from verification (e13 = the full user-facing review):
- The four biggest "delete" targets — `.entire/` (217 MB), `graphify-out/` (26 MB), `.superpowers/`, — are **already gitignored and untracked** (0 tracked files each). `.gitignore` explicitly lists `/.entire/`, `graphify-out/`, `/.superpowers/`, `/.claude/`, `/.codex/`, and LaTeX build artifacts + `/main.pdf`. `logs/` has 12 tracked files; `codetections_polarization/` has 29 tracked files.
- Line counts honest: `run_dsa_lorentzian_fits.py` 1330 (claimed 1297), `revalidation.py` 465, `flits/` 6379 total, `vo/` 6143 total, `priors_physical.py` 401, `config_utils.py` 321.
- `flits/` is imported by ~10 modules (not YAGNI). `vo/` not imported by runtime pipeline (only pyvo test). `config_utils.resolve_path` proposed stdlib replacement is wrong — `pathlib.Path` has no `.expandvars()`. `priors_physical` is a package-level export + lazy import + test ref (NE2001/NE2025 prior). `codetections_polarization/` is a genuinely separate paper (own `main.tex`+`main.pdf`).
- Total pipeline python LOC: 84396 (so -14,200 ≈ 17%).

**Auditor finding (e14), acknowledged (e15):** agent wrongly grouped `_trash/` as "already in .gitignore" — it is NOT in `.gitignore`; it is merely uncommitted (0 tracked files). Corrected: the three gitignored dirs are `.entire/`, `graphify-out/`, `.superpowers/`; `_trash/` is uncommitted only.

## Phase 2 — .git bloat investigation (e16–e28)
USER (e17): "Use this as a jumping off point for your own investigation into how to improve and cleanup the codebase. Which tasks do you see as most strongly advisable?"

`.git` is 1.2 GB. Breakdown: `.git/modules/pipeline` 530 MB (legitimate submodule object DB), `.git/worktrees` 503 MB, `.git/objects` 131 MB (pack 130.41 MiB, 5419 in-pack, size-garbage 17.97 KiB, 16 `tmp_obj_*` loose files ~68K). Largest historical blobs: repeated `.entire/metadata/*/full.jsonl` (7–11 MB each), `graphify-out/.graphify_ast.json` 6.7 MB, `docs/rse/specs/dm-power-h17-manifest-dynamic-spectra-sheet*.png` 6.2–6.3 MB. `.entire/graphify/.superpowers` NOT in HEAD tree (only history).

**The 503 MB anomaly:** one stale worktree admin dir `.git/worktrees/faber2026-jointmodel-pr-reconcile/modules` holds an orphaned duplicate submodule object store (siblings are ~52–60 KB). Six/seven `/private/tmp/` worktrees exist (all clean, 0 dirty lines): `faber-rebase` (branch `rebase-pcc`), `faber2026-dsa-subband-push-AyfHga` (`integrate/dsa-subband-20260708`), `faber2026-jointmodel-pr-reconcile` (detached), `faber2026-pr27-review-P8DrPM` (`fix/pr27-review-feedback`), `faber2026-push-clarify-qLV9A7` (`integrate/dsa-acf-push-20260708`), `faber2026-push-v6` (detached), and (appearing in e32) `faber2026-language-pass-LXMWWK` (`fix/manuscript-language-pass`).

Agent's prioritized recommendation (e26): (1) reclaim 503 MB stale worktree via `git worktree remove`; (2) `git gc` loose garbage; (3) defer `.entire/`-in-history purge (`git filter-repo`, rewrites 456 SHAs) to pre-submission; (4) safe janitorial: delete `_trash/`, dedup Lorentzian figures, split `codetections_polarization/`; (5) add reproducibility spine. Explicitly NOT: refactor `flits/`/`scattering/pipeline/`, merge `revalidation.py`, inline `priors_physical.py`/`config_utils`.

**Auditor finding (e27), acknowledged (e28):** agent contradicted itself by calling the Lorentzian figure-dedup a safe non-submodule-crossing win — those figures live at `pipeline/analysis/scintillation-dsa-lorentzian-2026-07-07/results/figures/`, INSIDE the submodule. Corrected: only `_trash/` and the `codetections_polarization/` split are true Faber2026-local janitorial wins.

## Phase 3 — safe task execution (e29–e43)
USER (e30): "Proceed with safe tasks now."

Two things changed since prior inspection, both making git-level tasks unsafe: (a) `git gc` failed with `Another git process seems to be running` — active lock on `refs/heads/entire/83f0041-e3b0c4`; (b) live concurrent git activity (worktree set mutating, HEAD moving, coarse-mode git protection because grant spans many repos). So worktree reclaim + gc were **deferred**.

Only safe action taken: `_trash/` moved to Trash via `delete_host_files` (path `/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/_trash`; held 11 zero-byte `tmp*.tmp` + 510-byte `PROVENANCE.md`). Confirmed removed, 0 tracked.

**Auditor finding (e40):** claimed the "faber2026-language-pass worktree appeared" between inspections was false. Agent **rebutted (e41)**: the first worktree listing (two turns earlier, before this span) had only 6 `/tmp` worktrees without `faber2026-language-pass`; it first appears in this turn's porcelain listing, so it genuinely appeared between the two reads (HEAD also moved `ad55909`→`741d263`). Rebuttal stands.

USER (e42): "What does `gc` do?" → agent explained git garbage collection (packs loose objects, prunes unreachable objects older than 2-week `gc.pruneExpire`, clears cruft; non-destructive to reachable work; won't shrink `.entire`-in-history because those blobs are still reachable — needs `git filter-repo`).

## Phase 4 — reproducibility spine (e44–e72, corrected e96–e118)
USER (e45): "Let's move to the reproducibility spine, I'll worry about `gc` later."

Traced every manuscript output → producing command by reading actual writer lines. LaTeX structure: `main.tex` `\input`s `sections/{intro,observations,methods,results,discussion,conclusions,appendix}.tex`; `\graphicspath{{figures/}}`. Table `\input` lines: `sections/observations.tex:17 \input{sample_table.tex}`, `sections/observations.tex:199 \input{foreground_table}`, `sections/results.tex:78 \input{budget_table}`. `beta_table.tex` NOT `\input` anywhere (staged, awaiting beta SLOT). Manuscript is WIP with bracketed abstract "SLOT" placeholders; only 9 outputs currently embedded, ~15 figures staged in `figures/`. Directly `\includegraphics`'d: `clusters_icm.pdf`, `dm_host_posteriors.pdf`, `ne2025_mw_characterization_nside32.pdf`, `sightline_halo_grid.pdf`, plus macro-driven `association_cards/association_card_#1.pdf` and `jointmodel_pair/#1_jointmodel_pair.pdf`.

First manifest built (e62–e63): 24 rows. Files written to repo root then copied to workspace and saved as artifacts (v1): `repro_manifest.csv` artifact_id `0adc8e2c-fb19-422e-b884-f04fe6987362` version_id `46a5bfc9-2563-4606-91a2-12efbfa6d6cf`; `REPRODUCE.md` artifact_id `6e7fea18-69d6-4ae1-8c81-d829efe77d69` version_id `e48aac8e-6f45-411a-8965-10e448b4ac89`.

**Auditor (e96) found 3 issues; all valid, fixed in v2 (e97–e115):**
1. Missing figure row: `figures/ne2025_mw_characterization.pdf` (plain, default `--nside 8`) was omitted — it's the orphan sibling of the embedded `_nside32` variant, same producer.
2. `budget_table.tex` producer wrong. Auditor suggested `sightline_budget.py:1107` but that actually writes `sightline_dm_scattering_budget.md` (**markdown**, `md_path` at L1104), NOT the `.tex`; `dm_budget_uncertainty.py` only mentions the filename in comments. Plan doc `docs/rse/specs/plan-trust-reset-revalidation.md` confirms `budget_table.tex` is CURRENTLY a hand transcription (emitter planned, not built). So it's a **third hand-maintained hazard** → `writer_verified=hand`.
3. `writer_verified` rubric inconsistency — `foreground_table.tex` marked `yes` despite no write line → fixed with explicit `hand`/`unresolved` values.

**v2 manifest = 25 rows** (21 figures + 4 tables). Tally: `yes`=9, `candidate`=13, `hand`=2 (`foreground_table.tex`, `budget_table.tex`), `unresolved`=1 (`whitney_multiplicity.pdf`). v2 artifacts saved as new versions: `repro_manifest.csv` version_id `5a23c8f3-d84e-4e1e-867f-2fd3020b9deb` (v2, 7294 bytes) under artifact `0adc8e2c-fb19-422e-b884-f04fe6987362`; `REPRODUCE.md` version_id `095950f0-c417-49ef-b6b0-161102824fc8` (v2, 6913 bytes) under artifact `6e7fea18-69d6-4ae1-8c81-d829efe77d69`.

### Producer resolutions (the two "mystery" figures)
- **freya_dsa_gamma_summary.pdf** (freya = FRB 20230325A): RESOLVED to producing function `plot_subband_gamma_summary` in `pipeline/scintillation/scint_analysis/plotting.py` (def at L1252; docstring "Reconstruction of the freya DSA summary-figure design"). Confirmed by commit `18fbe98` ("figs: freya DSA gamma(nu) summary — manuscript export from pinned pipeline"; body: `plot_subband_gamma_summary (FLITS #130) run against pinned submodule at 25b8cc6, freya_dsa.yaml stored Lorentzian fits; gammas match arm-J oracle exactly (917/1190/1164/1421 kHz)`). Matplotlib PDF metadata v3.10.8. Marked `candidate` (function has caller-supplied `save_path`, no committed CLI driver passes the stem). Configs exist at `pipeline/scintillation/configs/bursts/freya_dsa.yaml`.
- **whitney_multiplicity.pdf** (whitney = FRB 20220310F): UNRESOLVED. Committed as binary copy-in (commit `438825f` "docs(manuscript): reconcile scattering narrative to the all-exp/multicomponent fits", alongside `alpha_pbf_systematic.pdf`). Matplotlib metadata v3.10.6. Framed by commit `b96aa29` ("results: frame whitney 1-component demo as the [1.5,6.0]-prior illustration"; α rails to 1.5; whitney stays 2-component canonical α≈5.1). Likely local/HPC burstfit run never committed. ADR-0002 says such figures "should derive labels from `burst_metadata`, not hard-code them." Burst nickname map (`pipeline/analysis/scattering-refit-2026-06/joint_ladder/_subband_tau_validation.py`): `wilhelm`=FRB 20221203A, `freya`=FRB 20230325A, `whitney_fine`=FRB 20220310F. USER (e120) later confirmed: "I can't remember where I ran Whitney's single component demo" → stays unresolved.

### Two hazards recorded in spine
1. Two hand-maintained tables (`foreground_table.tex`, `budget_table.tex`) — no generators. `budget_table.tex`'s upstream: `sightline_budget.py` emits `.md`/`.csv` (not `.tex`); `dm_budget_uncertainty.py` supplies skew-corrected uncertainties + `dm_host_posteriors` fig. `language_audit.md` already caught a DR8/DR9 drift in `budget_table.tex`.
2. `plot_association_cards.py` (in `pipeline/crossmatching/`) hardcoded `MANUSCRIPT_OUTDIR` — FIXED this span (see Phase 5).

## Phase 5 — association-cards path fix (e73–e81, e116–e118)
USER (e73): "The hardcoded path should be replaced with `/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/figures/association_cards`". Original (L40): `MANUSCRIPT_OUTDIR = Path("/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/overleaf/Faber2026/figures/association_cards")`. `edit_file` replaced it with the repo-local path. Background bash check (e117) confirmed: line updated, `figures/association_cards` exists, script does `MANUSCRIPT_OUTDIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)` (L281) then `shutil.copy2(out, MANUSCRIPT_OUTDIR / out.name)` (L290) — works out of box. `OUTDIR = HERE / "association_cards"` (L39), `savefig` L288.

## Phase 6 — budget_table.tex emitter + parity test (e119–e123, span end mid-work)
USER (e120): "I can't remember where I ran Whitney's single component demo, but yes, please draft the `budget_table.tex` emitter and parity test against `siteline_budget.py`'s outputs." Session was resumed (e121) — kernel state reset.

Plan spec (`docs/rse/specs/plan-trust-reset-revalidation.md` P0.4): implement `format_budget_table_tex()` adjacent to `format_budget_table` (L837), reuse row assembly, emit deluxetable markup verbatim, `--emit-tex` flag, write to `exports/budget_table.tex`, parity test `tests/test_budget_table_emitter.py`.

Design decision forced by data: NO single machine-readable source reproduces the table — `dm_budget_uncertainty.py`'s `SIGHTLINES` dict has only 9/12 rows (3 placeholder-z excluded: freya/mahi/johndoeii), its CSV supplies DM_host posteriors, nothing carries `regime`/`tau_int`/footnote markers, pipeline budget CSV is stale. So emitter promotes 12 rows into a structured single-source file + renders markup.

Deliverables built and all verified (details in later conversation, not this span's end): parsed `budget_table.tex` 12 rows into structured fields; emitter reproduced all 12 rows byte-exact (whitespace-normalized); DM_host `median^{+p84}_{-p16}` cross-checked exactly against `scripts/dm_budget_uncertainty.csv` for all 9 non-placeholder sightlines. Files: `pipeline/galaxies/foreground/budget_table_data.json`, `pipeline/galaxies/foreground/budget_table_emitter.py`, `pipeline/galaxies/foreground/test_budget_table_emitter.py`, `pipeline/exports/budget_table.tex`, plus thin re-export `format_budget_table_tex()` added to `sightline_budget.py` (L1124).

At span end (e123), agent was reading `budget_table.tex` (full, 82 lines) and `plan-trust-reset-revalidation.md` P0.4 spec — the start of the emitter work.

Note (e101): FRB 20230307A is `regime=CGM, mass=assumed` in `budget_table.tex` but `dm_budget_uncertainty.py`'s SIGHTLINES lists it `mass="cluster"` — a discrepancy in the manuscript to reconcile.

## Deferred / open threads
- **Deferred (needs quiet repo):** 503 MB stale-worktree reclamation (`git worktree remove`) + `git gc`, blocked by concurrent git activity + coarse-mode protection. Suggested: user run directly, or narrow host grant to just this repo.
- **Pre-submission:** `.entire/`-in-history purge via `git filter-repo` (rewrites 456 SHAs).
- **Submodule-scoped (dsa110-FLITS):** Lorentzian PNG/SVG dedup.
- **Faber2026-local:** split `codetections_polarization/` to own repo.
- **whitney_multiplicity.pdf** producer permanently unresolved (user can't recall).
- Agent offered: apply emitter pattern to `foreground_table.tex`; wire manuscript `\input` to consume generated exports; commit the freya driver.

- read_file (e4) ponytail audit: version_id="0be6c6f1-141d-4808-9928-3e81ef2cbc05", size_bytes=5257, net_lines="~-14,200", artifact_bloat_MB=240
- bash top-level (e6): git_dir=1.2G, pipeline=595M, dot_entire=217M, figures=31M, graphify_out=26M, docs=24M, codetections_polarization=13M, logs=5.2M, superpowers=3.6M
- bash gitignore/tracking (e8): entire_tracked=0, graphify_tracked=0, logs_tracked=12, superpowers_tracked=0, codetections_tracked=29, trash_tracked=0, total_tracked_files=259

Decisions:
- trash_dir = "delete _trash/ (11 empty tmp stubs)" [repo, ruling, by:agent] (src: <83ec3cd6>)
- git_worktree_gc = "deferred — live concurrent git activity + gc lock" [repo, process, by:agent] (src: <ab704402>)
- assoc_card_path = "/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/figures/association_cards" [pipeline/crossmatching/plot_association_cards.py, param, by:user] (src: <b986f7c8>)
- reproducibility_spine = "trace every manuscript output back to its producing command" [repo, design, by:user] (src: <d2689e89>)
- writer_verified_rubric = "yes/candidate/hand/unresolved" [repro_manifest.csv, design, by:agent] (src: <7d6cd4c1>)
- whitney_producer = "unresolved — committed as binary, no producer in tree" [repro_manifest.csv, ruling, by:user] (src: <6cbff419>)
